response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has a strong generalist software engineering background spanning over 15 years, but he is critically mismatched for this role. The single most disqualifying factor is the absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is listed as a hard requirement. Beyond that, his experience is almost entirely in web, mobile, and cloud-native development (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB), with no demonstrated work in embedded networking, network security protocols, Layer-2/3 switching, or Linux kernel development. His C/C++ exposure appears limited to a brief stint at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project, neither of which aligns with the depth required for embedded security firmware engineering.
strengths: Long career (15+ years) demonstrating adaptability across web, mobile, embedded, and systems programming contexts
Some C/C++ and embedded-adjacent experience from National Instruments and robotic-arm work at Altom Consulting
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) that would transfer to any engineering role
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a hard, non-negotiable requirement for this position
Zero experience in network security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) or Layer-2/3 networking and switch architecture
No evidence of Linux kernel investigation, source-level modification, or embedded firmware development in a networking context

key_factors.factor: No network security or embedded networking experience
Breadth of programming languages including C
